topic: Someone finds their college application essay published as an example
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elias traced the glossy paper with a fingertip, the edges crisp and undeniably official. It was his essay, his carefully crafted narrative about navigating a difficult summer internship, now displayed on a screen under the banner of "Successful Applicants." A cold knot tightened in his stomach, pulling at his diaphragm. He reread the opening paragraph, the words feeling suddenly alien, detached from the genuine struggle he’d poured onto the page. His hands began to tremble, and he quickly shoved the laptop away, the sudden movement jarring. He needed air. The silence of his dorm room amplified the frantic drumming in his ears. He stared at the screen, the perfect prose mocking his own clumsy attempts at articulation.

[story 2]

The discovery hit Maya like a physical blow. Tucked between a stack of library books was the printed version of her personal statement, featured prominently in the admissions portal’s 'Best Examples' section. Her breath hitched, shallow and quick. She picked it up, the paper warm beneath her fingers, and her vision blurred slightly. A flush crept up her neck, staining her cheeks a deep rose. *This isn't mine. This isn't the story I meant to tell.* She dropped it onto the worn wooden desk with a soft thud, the sound echoing loudly in the quiet apartment. She stood up abruptly, pacing the small space, her heels clicking a hurried rhythm against the floorboards, trying to force her racing pulse to slow down.

[story 3]

When Liam found the file, he froze mid-sip of his coffee. The screen glowed with the familiar layout of the application review page, but the text displayed was undeniably his own reflection, polished and presented as an ideal. A sudden wave of dizziness washed over him; he gripped the mug so tightly his knuckles turned white. He blinked rapidly, trying to reorient himself to the reality of the situation. Every muscle in his jaw felt suddenly locked, and he swallowed hard, the dryness in his throat making the action difficult. He backed away from the computer, his gaze darting around the room as if expecting the document to suddenly move or change.
